programs.name: Golf: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In golf, you will learn basics like stance, swing, putt and drive as well as game etiquette through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts. Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Music: Guitar
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the guitar with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Tri Private
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Music: Keyboard - Advanced
programs.description: In advanced music you will build on fun and fundamentals and work towards refinement. Here you will continue to practice proper hand positions and basic scales and learn more complex rhythms and the use of foot pedals. As you continue to practice you will work towards performing more complex pieces.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop pickleball play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, pickleball offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Swim Fit
programs.description: The course name says it all—Swim Fit. In this activity, you’ll not only get instruction on technique and stroke improvement, but you’ll also get the opportunity to perform interval drills to increase your swimming performance and endurance.Prerequisite: Must be able to swim two continuous lengths of the pool or 50 metres.

programs.name: Swim Fit
programs.description: The course name says it all—Swim Fit. In this activity, you’ll not only get instruction on technique and stroke improvement, but you’ll also get the opportunity to perform interval drills to increase your swimming performance and endurance.Prerequisite: Must be able to swim two continuous lengths of the pool or 50 metres.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Tai Chi
programs.description: Embrace the body and mind through the practice of tai chi . Learn how to pair gentle movements that you will perform in a sequence with controlled breathing, to improve your flexibility, coordination, strength and mental concentration.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Aikido - Advanced
programs.description: Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.  They will learn fundamentals of competition and perform simple fighting skills, and demonstrate exemplary etiquette.

programs.name: Aikido - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Alterations in Fashion and Home Interiors
programs.description: Transform clothing into custom fitted pieces. You will learn how to do alterations such as repairs to torn clothing, reducing or increasing the size of a garment, lengthening or shortening sleeves, hems, replacing the lining in a jacket or coat, redesigning a garment, replacing a zipper, adding buttonholes and buttons, etc. Alterations for home décor will include lengthening and shortening drapery, making slip covers for chairs or small sofas and re-upholstering seat covers and pillows. This course is designed to teach to the strengths of the individual learner, beginners welcome. In house sewing machines available. Students must bring their own fabrics and notions. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Mind and Body
programs.description: Combine the calm of an aquatic environment with mindful movements. Focusing on balance and breath, elements from Pilates, tai chi and yoga will be incorporated into this workout.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Water Walking
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Water Walking Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. The water walking program reviews ways to improve gait and provides a variety of stretching, strengthening and cardiovascular conditioning exercises. Learning a variety of walking patterns and progressions will provide additional core, posture, balance, and mobility benefits.Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. This certification provides instructors with the skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in shallow water. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.Prerequisite: Current Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification (Shallow/Deep)For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aikido - Advanced
programs.description: Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.  They will learn fundamentals of competition and perform simple fighting skills, and demonstrate exemplary etiquette.

programs.name: Aikido - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Alterations in Fashion and Home Interiors
programs.description: Transform clothing into custom fitted pieces. You will learn how to do alterations such as repairs to torn clothing, reducing or increasing the size of a garment, lengthening or shortening sleeves, hems, replacing the lining in a jacket or coat, redesigning a garment, replacing a zipper, adding buttonholes and buttons, etc. Alterations for home décor will include lengthening and shortening drapery, making slip covers for chairs or small sofas and re-upholstering seat covers and pillows. This course is designed to teach to the strengths of the individual learner, beginners welcome. In house sewing machines available. Students must bring their own fabrics and notions. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Mind and Body
programs.description: Combine the calm of an aquatic environment with mindful movements. Focusing on balance and breath, elements from Pilates, tai chi and yoga will be incorporated into this workout.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Water Walking
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Water Walking Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. The water walking program reviews ways to improve gait and provides a variety of stretching, strengthening and cardiovascular conditioning exercises. Learning a variety of walking patterns and progressions will provide additional core, posture, balance, and mobility benefits.Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. This certification provides instructors with the skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in shallow water. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.Prerequisite: Current Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification (Shallow/Deep)For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Aikido - Advanced
programs.description: Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.  They will learn fundamentals of competition and perform simple fighting skills, and demonstrate exemplary etiquette.

programs.name: Aikido - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Alterations in Fashion and Home Interiors
programs.description: Transform clothing into custom fitted pieces. You will learn how to do alterations such as repairs to torn clothing, reducing or increasing the size of a garment, lengthening or shortening sleeves, hems, replacing the lining in a jacket or coat, redesigning a garment, replacing a zipper, adding buttonholes and buttons, etc. Alterations for home décor will include lengthening and shortening drapery, making slip covers for chairs or small sofas and re-upholstering seat covers and pillows. This course is designed to teach to the strengths of the individual learner, beginners welcome. In house sewing machines available. Students must bring their own fabrics and notions. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Mind and Body
programs.description: Combine the calm of an aquatic environment with mindful movements. Focusing on balance and breath, elements from Pilates, tai chi and yoga will be incorporated into this workout.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Water Walking
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Water Walking Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. The water walking program reviews ways to improve gait and provides a variety of stretching, strengthening and cardiovascular conditioning exercises. Learning a variety of walking patterns and progressions will provide additional core, posture, balance, and mobility benefits.Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. This certification provides instructors with the skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in shallow water. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.Prerequisite: Current Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification (Shallow/Deep)For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Adapted Recreation
programs.description: Join the fun with Adapted Recreation. Take part in activities such as arts and crafts, sports, fitness and community outings while enjoying the company of new friends.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Computers: Web Design
programs.description: Have you ever thought about designing your very own webpage? Combining technical elements and artistic design, instruction will focus on the application of basic web design standards. Participants should have basic computer skills and understand how to navigate the internet.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Dance: Basics
programs.description: Combining influences of lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip hop and modern dance this activity is the perfect for beginners. Learn basic positions, body awareness and proper technique through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts. Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational multi-sport. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Music: Drumming - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the drum with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn grip, stroke, rhythm and hand and foot coordination.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the guitar with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Piano
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to piano. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Club: Dungeons and Dragons
programs.description: Are you ready to make the next move? In this activity, you will play Dungeons and Dragons. This activity is facilitated by staff, there is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Science: Building Adventures
programs.description: Keep little hands busy with building. Stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play using different materials and tools helps explore how to construct objects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray (Girls)
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres. This activity is for girls only.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Intermediate
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.
